welcome the opportunity to show you how the platform could support your

Would you be open to a brief virtual meeting or call to explore this
further?

bryan.matthews@hayward-ca.gov.original_subject: The trail goes cold before your team sees it
bryan.matthews@hayward-ca.gov.original_body:
Chief Matthews —

You've spent 25 years building Hayward. When organized retail crime forces
stores to close, that's not just a policing problem — it's your community
shrinking.

Your investigators handle what happens in the store. But the networks
behind it are selling online within hours — across Amazon, eBay, Facebook —
and that trail disappears fast.

Cluster Forensics follows it. We connect physical theft to the organized
networks fencing it online and build the evidence prosecutors need to
actually convict.

We're working with CVS Health and federal LE partners. Worth 20 minutes?


Marco,

retailers and law
enforcement agencies to detect stolen goods being resold on online
marketplaces and map the
organized crime networks behind them.

What makes our platform different from traditional investigative tools:
• We connect in-store theft events to online marketplace resale activity in

what was stolen to where it’s being sold and by whom

orc_news.content: + Emerging Trends in Large-Scale Retail Theft. Unlike traditional shoplifting, ORC involves coordinated theft rings that target businesses with strategic precision, often stealing high-value merchandise in bulk and reselling it through online marketplaces or underground networks. ORC costs U.S. retailers billions each year, with recent reports showing a sharp increase in large-scale theft incidents. pubsfty@sunnyvale.ca.gov.original_subject: Retail Crime Intelligence — Supporting ORC Investigations
pubsfty@sunnyvale.ca.gov.original_body:
Hi Chief Barberini,


I’m reaching out because our platform may be directly relevant to the types
of organized retail crime cases your team investigates.


Cluster Forensics is a Retail Crime Intelligence Platform used by major
retailers and law

enforcement agencies to detect stolen goods being resold on online
marketplaces and map the

organized crime networks behind them.


What makes our platform different from traditional investigative tools:

• We connect in-store theft events to online marketplace resale activity in
real time — linking

what was stolen to where it’s being sold and by whom


• Our intelligence identifies and maps seller networks across multiple
marketplaces, revealing

the scale and structure of ORC operations


• The platform provides forensic-grade evidence preservation that supports
investigation and

prosecution


• We deliver intelligence briefings tailored to your jurisdiction — so your
team can see exactly

which organized sellers are operating in your area and the scale of their
activity


We’re currently working with several law enforcement agencies and would
welcome the opportunity to show you how the platform could support your
team’s work.


Would you be open to a brief virtual meeting or call to explore this
further?


[image: Cluster Forensics] <https://clusterforensics.com/>

Marco Faccin
Head of Growth

# Police Chief vs Sheriff: Understanding the Key Differences. **So, what is the difference between a police chief and a sheriff?**. **The primary difference is that a police chief is the highest-ranking officer of a municipal police department appointed by city officials, while a sheriff is an elected county official who oversees law enforcement and other duties across the entire county jurisdiction.**. Hi Chief Mark,

I’m reaching out because our platform may be directly relevant to the types
of organized retail crime cases your team investigates.

Cluster Forensics is a Retail Crime Intelligence Platform used by major
retailers and law
enforcement agencies to detect stolen goods being resold on online
marketplaces and map the
organized crime networks behind them.

What makes our platform different from traditional investigative tools:
• We connect in-store theft events to online marketplace resale activity in
real time — linking
what was stolen to where it’s being sold and by whom

• Our intelligence identifies and maps seller networks across multiple
marketplaces, revealing
the scale and structure of ORC operations

• The platform provides forensic-grade evidence preservation that supports
investigation and
prosecution

• We deliver intelligence briefings tailored to your jurisdiction — so your
team can see exactly
which organized sellers are operating in your area and the scale of their
activity

We’re currently working with several law enforcement agencies and would
welcome the opportunity to show you how the platform could support your
team’s work.

Would you be open to a brief virtual meeting or call to explore this
further?
